parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Wide Right Zone
context.name: WR
context.definition: The wide-right zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the RB zone and in front the in-behind right zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside right behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Wide Left Zone
context.name: WL
context.definition: The wide-left zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the LB zone and in front the in-behind left zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside left behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Centre-Mid Zone
context.name: CM
context.definition: The CM zone is the central and semi-central space within the opponent's midfield, located ahead of the DM zone & behind the AM zone. Vertically, it is located behind the opposing strikers and first midfielders, but not yet directly in front of the last line of defence. Horizontally, it is located between the two wings of the opponent's formation.
context.meaning: Central space ahead of the DM zone and before the AM zone.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Attacking-Mid Zone
context.name: AM
context.definition: The AM zone is the central and semi-central zone immediately in front of the opponent's last line of defence. It is bordered to the back by the CM zone & to the front by the in-behind central zone. Horizontally, it is located inside the opponent's formation. Laterally, it borders the in-behind wide zones.
context.meaning: Central zone directly in front of the opponent's defensive line.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: In-Behind Wide Right Zone
context.name: IBWR
context.definition: The in-behind right zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the right side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide right zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: In-Behind Wide Left Zone
context.name: IBWL
context.definition: The in-behind left zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the left side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide left zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: In-Behind Central Zone
context.name: IB
context.definition: The in-behind central zone is the area behind the opponent's defence. An action here often represents a complete breakthrough on goal. There is a maximum of one opposing centre-back and the opposing goalkeeper between the ball and the goal. Vertically, you are within the boundaries of the opponent's formation. Laterally, the zone reaches the two furthest points of the defence (generally each full-back). To the rear, it borders the AM zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a central space with at least one central defender bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Own Box
context.name: OWN_BOX
context.definition: The penalty box (16-metre zone) of the team currently in possession of the ball.
context.meaning: Own area

parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).

context.name: FIRST_THIRD
context.definition: The first third of the pitch as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball. The box is not included here.
context.meaning: First third of the pitch without the box.

parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.definition: The middle third of the pitch.
context.meaning: Middle third of the pitch.

parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).

context.label: Final Third
context.name: FINAL_THIRD
context.definition: The final third of the pitch as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball. The opposition box is not included here.
context.meaning: Final third of the pitch without an the opponent’s box.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Opponent Box

context.definition: The opponent's box (16-metre zone) as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball.

context.label: In Possession Phase
context.name: IN_POSSESSION
context.definition: In Possession phase starts when a team has the ball in a zone at the back (GK, FB or CB zone) for at least 4 seconds. The possession phase ends when the opponent wins the ball, there is a second ball, a set-piece follows, or there is any other interruption in play.
context.meaning: Controlled build-up of an attack.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Attacking Transition Phase
context.name: ATTACKING_TRANSITION
context.definition: Attacking Transition phases of play start when possession of the ball is won where there has been some control of the ball (pressure <40). It ends as soon as the team decides to take speed out of the situation (see possession phase), there is a second ball, a set-piece follows or there is any other interruption to play.
context.meaning: Direct attack after winning the ball without having brought the ball under complete control.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Set Piece Phase
context.name: SET_PIECE
context.definition: Set piece phases start with the taking of a set piece (throw-in, corner, free kick, goalkeeper kick, penalty kick). While throw-ins, penalty kicks and goalkeeper kicks end after being taken, the set piece phase for corners and free kicks runs until an action with little opponent pressure takes place or the ball has been transported out of the final third. A direct header after a corner therefore also falls into the standard phase.
context.meaning: Attack through a set piece, especially after free kicks and corners.

parentKpi.label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
parentKpi.name: BYPASSED_OPPONENTS_RECEIVING_NUMBER
parentKpi.definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).
parentKpi.meaning: Number of actions in which a player is found as a receiver.

context.label: Second Ball Phase
context.name: SECOND_BALL
context.definition: Second Ball phase of the game begins after uncontrollable or extremely hard to control actions, e.g. an aerial duel or blocked pass/shot, when both teams are then in a fight for the ball. You cannot clearly assign possession of the ball to either of the two teams in these phases. The phase ends when one team manages to bring enough control over the ball (pressure <40) or there is a stoppage of play.
context.meaning: Phase in which neither team manages to keep full control of the ball. 'Chaos phase.'

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Availability Between the Lines
context.name: AVAILABILITY_BTL
context.definition: This action describes receiving the ball in one of the following zones: DM zone, CM zone, AM zone.
context.meaning: Willingness to get on the ball in the midfield. Show in between the lines

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Availability Out Wide
context.name: AVAILABILITY_OUT_WIDE
context.definition: This action describes receiving the ball in one of the following zones: Wide Left, Wide Right, LB Zone, RB Zone
context.meaning: Willingness to get on the ball in wide areas.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Availability From Deep Runs
context.name: AVAILABILITY_FDR
context.definition: Receiving the ball behind the opponent's defensive chain. The ball must therefore be received in one of the three zones behind the defence (in-behind left, in-behind right, in-behind central), and the pass must be made from one of the zones in front.
context.meaning: Option in-behind, deep runner, getting in behind the opponent's defence

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Availability in the Back
context.name: AVAILABILITY_IN_THE_BACK
context.definition: This skill availability at the back illustrates receiving the ball in defensive areas after a pass from a teammate when the ball is received in one of the following zones: GK zone, CB zone, FB zone or DM zone. (A maximum of one opponent has been bypassed in these areas).
context.meaning: Willingness to get on the ball at the back

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Availability in the Box
context.name: AVAILABILITY_IN_THE_BOX
context.definition: A player receives a pass in the opponent's box.
context.meaning: Ball contacts in the opponent's box, presence in the opponent's box.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Block
context.name: BLOCK
context.definition: An action is considered blocked if a player touches the ball under such high pressure that he has no possibility of controlling the ball (blocking the shot, deflecting it, being shot at, etc…).
context.meaning: Blocked shots, blocked passes, blocked crosses

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Header
context.name: HEADER
context.definition: The action header includes all receptions and releases of the ball that are made with the head. This includes passes with the head and goal finishes with the head.
context.meaning: Header

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Hold-Up Play
context.name: HOLD_UP_PLAY
context.definition: A player receives & retains possession from a high pass under pressure or a low pass where he has to hold the ball in a one-on-one situation.
context.meaning: Control and secure high balls, maintain possession from a flat pass with the opponent at your back.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Loose Ball Regain
context.name: LOOSE_BALL_REGAIN
context.definition: Winning "free" balls on the pitch, which do not include intercepted passes, clearances or tackles. This occurs mainly after second balls, such as aerial duels and from bouncing balls
context.meaning: Win second balls, anticipation, being combative and hardworking

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Full-Back Right Zone
context.name: FBR
context.definition: The RB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the RB zone borders on the wide right zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the right of the CB zone where the outside right player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Centre-Back Zone
context.name: CB
context.definition: The CB zone is located in front of the opposing forwards, usually with all opponents between the ball and the opposing goal. It borders laterally the two FB zones, depending on whether one is inside or outside the opponent's formation. To the front, the CB zone borders on the DM zone, to the back on the GK zone.
context.meaning: Neutral zone in which play is built from the centre.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Full-Back Left Zone
context.name: FBL
context.definition: The LB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the LB zone borders on the wide left zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the left of the CB zone where the outside left player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Defensive-Mid Zone
context.name: DM
context.definition: The DM zone is the central and semi-central zone behind the opposing strikers and in front of the opposing midfielders. It borders the back of the CB zone and the front of the CM zone. It is bordered to the sides by the two wide zone.
context.meaning: Centrally ahead of the opponent strikers/ first line of defence

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Wide Right Zone
context.name: WR
context.definition: The wide-right zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the RB zone and in front the in-behind right zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside right behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Wide Left Zone
context.name: WL
context.definition: The wide-left zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the LB zone and in front the in-behind left zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside left behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Centre-Mid Zone
context.name: CM
context.definition: The CM zone is the central and semi-central space within the opponent's midfield, located ahead of the DM zone & behind the AM zone. Vertically, it is located behind the opposing strikers and first midfielders, but not yet directly in front of the last line of defence. Horizontally, it is located between the two wings of the opponent's formation.
context.meaning: Central space ahead of the DM zone and before the AM zone.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Attacking-Mid Zone
context.name: AM
context.definition: The AM zone is the central and semi-central zone immediately in front of the opponent's last line of defence. It is bordered to the back by the CM zone & to the front by the in-behind central zone. Horizontally, it is located inside the opponent's formation. Laterally, it borders the in-behind wide zones.
context.meaning: Central zone directly in front of the opponent's defensive line.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: In-Behind Wide Right Zone
context.name: IBWR
context.definition: The in-behind right zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the right side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide right zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: In-Behind Wide Left Zone
context.name: IBWL
context.definition: The in-behind left zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the left side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide left zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: In-Behind Central Zone
context.name: IB
context.definition: The in-behind central zone is the area behind the opponent's defence. An action here often represents a complete breakthrough on goal. There is a maximum of one opposing centre-back and the opposing goalkeeper between the ball and the goal. Vertically, you are within the boundaries of the opponent's formation. Laterally, the zone reaches the two furthest points of the defence (generally each full-back). To the rear, it borders the AM zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a central space with at least one central defender bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Own Box
context.name: OWN_BOX
context.definition: The penalty box (16-metre zone) of the team currently in possession of the ball.
context.meaning: Own area

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: First Third
context.name: FIRST_THIRD
context.definition: The first third of the pitch as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball. The box is not included here.
context.meaning: First third of the pitch without the box.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Second Third
context.name: MIDDLE_THIRD
context.definition: The middle third of the pitch.
context.meaning: Middle third of the pitch.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Final Third
context.name: FINAL_THIRD
context.definition: The final third of the pitch as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball. The opposition box is not included here.
context.meaning: Final third of the pitch without an the opponent’s box.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Opponent Box
context.name: OPPONENT_BOX
context.definition: The opponent's box (16-metre zone) as seen by the team currently in possession of the ball.
context.meaning: Opposition area

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Right Wing
context.name: RIGHT_WING
context.definition: The outside right zone from the point of view of the team currently in possession of the ball. The far left hand side of this is bordered by the start of the opponent box.
context.meaning: Right wing of the pitch.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Right Half-Space
context.name: RIGHT_HALF_SPACE
context.definition: The half-space on the right from the point of view of the team currently in possession of the ball. This lane is bordered on the right by the edge of the box & to the left by the 6-yard-box.
context.meaning: Right half-space.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Centre
context.name: CENTER
context.definition: The centre of the pitch has the same width as the 6-yard-box.
context.meaning: Central lane of the pitch.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Left Half-Space
context.name: LEFT_HALF_SPACE
context.definition: The half-space on the left from the point of view of the team currently in possession of the ball. This lane is bordered on the left by the edge of the box & to the right by the 6-yard-box.
context.meaning: Left half-space.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Left Wing
context.name: LEFT_WING
context.definition: The outside left zone from the point of view of the team currently in possession of the ball. The far right hand side of this is bordered by the start of the opponent box.
context.meaning: Left wing of the pitch.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: In Possession Phase
context.name: IN_POSSESSION
context.definition: In Possession phase starts when a team has the ball in a zone at the back (GK, FB or CB zone) for at least 4 seconds. The possession phase ends when the opponent wins the ball, there is a second ball, a set-piece follows, or there is any other interruption in play.
context.meaning: Controlled build-up of an attack.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Attacking Transition Phase
context.name: ATTACKING_TRANSITION
context.definition: Attacking Transition phases of play start when possession of the ball is won where there has been some control of the ball (pressure <40). It ends as soon as the team decides to take speed out of the situation (see possession phase), there is a second ball, a set-piece follows or there is any other interruption to play.
context.meaning: Direct attack after winning the ball without having brought the ball under complete control.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Set Piece Phase
context.name: SET_PIECE
context.definition: Set piece phases start with the taking of a set piece (throw-in, corner, free kick, goalkeeper kick, penalty kick). While throw-ins, penalty kicks and goalkeeper kicks end after being taken, the set piece phase for corners and free kicks runs until an action with little opponent pressure takes place or the ball has been transported out of the final third. A direct header after a corner therefore also falls into the standard phase.
context.meaning: Attack through a set piece, especially after free kicks and corners.

parentKpi.label: Offensive Touches
parentKpi.name: OFFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in offensive play (when own team has been in possession).

context.label: Second Ball Phase
context.name: SECOND_BALL
context.definition: Second Ball phase of the game begins after uncontrollable or extremely hard to control actions, e.g. an aerial duel or blocked pass/shot, when both teams are then in a fight for the ball. You cannot clearly assign possession of the ball to either of the two teams in these phases. The phase ends when one team manages to bring enough control over the ball (pressure <40) or there is a stoppage of play.
context.meaning: Phase in which neither team manages to keep full control of the ball. 'Chaos phase.'

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Interception
context.name: INTERCEPTION
context.definition: An intercepted pass includes all the opponent's pass attempts that are picked up.
context.meaning: Intercept passes, anticipate the ball

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Ground Duel
context.name: DUEL
context.definition: The action ground duel describes the (temporary) winning of the ball through a duel. The opponent must not have attempted a pass, but start a dribble.
context.meaning: Direct duels, physical strength

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Loose Ball Regain
context.name: LOOSE_BALL_REGAIN
context.definition: Winning "free" balls on the pitch, which do not include intercepted passes, clearances or tackles. This occurs mainly after second balls, such as aerial duels and from bouncing balls
context.meaning: Win second balls, anticipation, being combative and hardworking

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Header
context.name: HEADER
context.definition: The action header includes all receptions and releases of the ball that are made with the head. This includes passes with the head and goal finishes with the head.
context.meaning: Header

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Block
context.name: BLOCK
context.definition: An action is considered blocked if a player touches the ball under such high pressure that he has no possibility of controlling the ball (blocking the shot, deflecting it, being shot at, etc…).
context.meaning: Blocked shots, blocked passes, blocked crosses

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Full-Back Right Zone
context.name: FBR
context.definition: The RB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the RB zone borders on the wide right zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the right of the CB zone where the outside right player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Centre-Back Zone
context.name: CB
context.definition: The CB zone is located in front of the opposing forwards, usually with all opponents between the ball and the opposing goal. It borders laterally the two FB zones, depending on whether one is inside or outside the opponent's formation. To the front, the CB zone borders on the DM zone, to the back on the GK zone.
context.meaning: Neutral zone in which play is built from the centre.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Full-Back Left Zone
context.name: FBL
context.definition: The LB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the LB zone borders on the wide left zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the left of the CB zone where the outside left player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Defensive-Mid Zone
context.name: DM
context.definition: The DM zone is the central and semi-central zone behind the opposing strikers and in front of the opposing midfielders. It borders the back of the CB zone and the front of the CM zone. It is bordered to the sides by the two wide zone.
context.meaning: Centrally ahead of the opponent strikers/ first line of defence

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Wide Right Zone
context.name: WR
context.definition: The wide-right zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the RB zone and in front the in-behind right zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside right behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES

parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.name: ATTACKING_TRANSITION
context.definition: Attacking Transition phases of play start when possession of the ball is won where there has been some control of the ball (pressure <40). It ends as soon as the team decides to take speed out of the situation (see possession phase), there is a second ball, a set-piece follows or there is any other interruption to play.
context.meaning: Direct attack after winning the ball without having brought the ball under complete control.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Set Piece Phase
context.name: SET_PIECE
context.definition: Set piece phases start with the taking of a set piece (throw-in, corner, free kick, goalkeeper kick, penalty kick). While throw-ins, penalty kicks and goalkeeper kicks end after being taken, the set piece phase for corners and free kicks runs until an action with little opponent pressure takes place or the ball has been transported out of the final third. A direct header after a corner therefore also falls into the standard phase.
context.meaning: Attack through a set piece, especially after free kicks and corners.

parentKpi.label: Defensive Touches
parentKpi.name: DEFENSIVE_TOUCHES
parentKpi.definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.
parentKpi.meaning: Ball contacts in defensive play (when opposition team has been in possession).

context.label: Second Ball Phase
context.name: SECOND_BALL
context.definition: Second Ball phase of the game begins after uncontrollable or extremely hard to control actions, e.g. an aerial duel or blocked pass/shot, when both teams are then in a fight for the ball. You cannot clearly assign possession of the ball to either of the two teams in these phases. The phase ends when one team manages to bring enough control over the ball (pressure <40) or there is a stoppage of play.
context.meaning: Phase in which neither team manages to keep full control of the ball. 'Chaos phase.'

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Low Pass
context.name: LOW_PASS
context.definition: A flat pass with the foot is when the trajectory of the ball during a pass reaches a maximum of chest height. The ball can land with a teammate, an opponent or out of bounds.
context.meaning: A flat pass with the foot.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Diagonal Pass
context.name: DIAGONAL_PASS
context.definition: A diagonal ball is a high ball played with the foot from the centre or out wide and switches of play to the wing. It is distinguished from the chipped/ lofted pass (as this is usually along the line or through the centre), the cross (usually into the box), a clearance (usually under very high pressure) and the short aerial pass (usually a short high passes).
context.meaning: High pass with the foot that changes play to the other side.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Chipped/Lofted Pass
context.name: CHIPPED_PASS
context.definition: A chipped or lofted pass is a high pass made from the foot where the ball travels a distance of at least 15 metres. These are mainly high passes along the line or in the centre of the pitch. It is therefore distinguished from the diagonal ball (usually out to the outside of the pitch), the high cross (usually into the box), a clearance (usually under very high pressure) and a short aerial pas (usually with pass length less than 15 metres).
context.meaning: High pass with the foot with a minimum length of 15 metres.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Short Aerial Pass
context.name: SHORT_AERIAL_PASS
context.definition: A short aerial pass is a high pass from the foot. These are predominantly short lobs, volleys or chips, e.g. as a result of a throw-in or after a bounce. It differs from the chipped/lofted pass primarily due to the length of the pass (<15 metres).
context.meaning: High pass with the foot with a maximum length of 15 metres.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Low Cross
context.name: LOW_CROSS
context.definition: A low cross is a cross into the opponent's box from wide. The ball must not reach more than chest height during it's trajectory, otherwise it is then counted as a high cross.
context.meaning: A low cross is a cross that is a low ball into the opponent's box from the wings.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: High Cross
context.name: HIGH_CROSS
context.definition: A high cross is an aerial cross into the opponent's box from wide. The ball must reach chest height at least once during the ball’s trajectory.
context.meaning: A high cross is a cross that is a high ball into the opponent's box from the wings.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Clearance with Foot
context.name: CLEARANCE
context.definition: A clearance is characterised by the need to relieve a high press situation by clearing the ball with a defensive action. These are most frequently clearances with only one contact on the ball.
context.meaning: A defensive action on the ball with the foot when under high pressure

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates

context.label: Header
context.name: HEADER
context.definition: The action header includes all receptions and releases of the ball that are made with the head. This includes passes with the head and goal finishes with the head.
context.meaning: Header

context.label: Block
context.name: BLOCK
context.definition: An action is considered blocked if a player touches the ball under such high pressure that he has no possibility of controlling the ball (blocking the shot, deflecting it, being shot at, etc…).
context.meaning: Blocked shots, blocked passes, blocked crosses

context.label: Save
context.name: SAVE
context.definition: The goalkeeper parries the ball without holding on to it
context.meaning: Parry by the goalkeeper, not caught

context.label: Full-Back Right Zone
context.name: FBR
context.definition: The RB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the RB zone borders on the wide right zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the right of the CB zone where the outside right player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Centre-Back Zone
context.name: CB
context.definition: The CB zone is located in front of the opposing forwards, usually with all opponents between the ball and the opposing goal. It borders laterally the two FB zones, depending on whether one is inside or outside the opponent's formation. To the front, the CB zone borders on the DM zone, to the back on the GK zone.
context.meaning: Neutral zone in which play is built from the centre.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Full-Back Left Zone
context.name: FBL
context.definition: The LB zone is adjacent to the CB zone and is defined by the player's position being outside of the opponent's formation. To the front, the LB zone borders on the wide left zone. The decisive factor is the number of opponents between the ball and the opposition goal.
context.meaning: Deep zone to the left of the CB zone where the outside left player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Defensive-Mid Zone
context.name: DM
context.definition: The DM zone is the central and semi-central zone behind the opposing strikers and in front of the opposing midfielders. It borders the back of the CB zone and the front of the CM zone. It is bordered to the sides by the two wide zone.
context.meaning: Centrally ahead of the opponent strikers/ first line of defence

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Wide Right Zone
context.name: WR
context.definition: The wide-right zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the RB zone and in front the in-behind right zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside right behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Wide Left Zone
context.name: WL
context.definition: The wide-left zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the LB zone and in front the in-behind left zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside left behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Centre-Mid Zone
context.name: CM
context.definition: The CM zone is the central and semi-central space within the opponent's midfield, located ahead of the DM zone & behind the AM zone. Vertically, it is located behind the opposing strikers and first midfielders, but not yet directly in front of the last line of defence. Horizontally, it is located between the two wings of the opponent's formation.
context.meaning: Central space ahead of the DM zone and before the AM zone.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Attacking-Mid Zone
context.name: AM
context.definition: The AM zone is the central and semi-central zone immediately in front of the opponent's last line of defence. It is bordered to the back by the CM zone & to the front by the in-behind central zone. Horizontally, it is located inside the opponent's formation. Laterally, it borders the in-behind wide zones.
context.meaning: Central zone directly in front of the opponent's defensive line.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: In-Behind Wide Right Zone
context.name: IBWR
context.definition: The in-behind right zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the right side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide right zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES

context.label: In-Behind Wide Left Zone

To the left side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide left zone.

parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: In-Behind Central Zone
context.name: IB
context.definition: The in-behind central zone is the area behind the opponent's defence. An action here often represents a complete breakthrough on goal. There is a maximum of one opposing centre-back and the opposing goalkeeper between the ball and the goal. Vertically, you are within the boundaries of the opponent's formation. Laterally, the zone reaches the two furthest points of the defence (generally each full-back). To the rear, it borders the AM zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a central space with at least one central defender bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates

context.meaning: Deep zone to the right of the CB zone where the outside right player in the defensive line is usually located.

parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Wide Right Zone
context.name: WR
context.definition: The wide-right zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the RB zone and in front the in-behind right zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside right behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Wide Left Zone
context.name: WL
context.definition: The wide-left zone is behind the opposing strikers and often also behind the opposing winger. It is the space to the outside behind the first defensive line and in front of the last defensive line of the opponent. Horizontally, you are outside the opponent's formation. Behind, the zone borders the LB zone and in front the in-behind left zone. Laterally, it borders both the DM zone and the CM zone & can at time also border the AM zone, depending on the opponent's formation & shape.
context.meaning: Space on the outside left behind the first line of defence and in front of the last line of defence of the opponent.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Centre-Mid Zone
context.name: CM
context.definition: The CM zone is the central and semi-central space within the opponent's midfield, located ahead of the DM zone & behind the AM zone. Vertically, it is located behind the opposing strikers and first midfielders, but not yet directly in front of the last line of defence. Horizontally, it is located between the two wings of the opponent's formation.
context.meaning: Central space ahead of the DM zone and before the AM zone.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: Attacking-Mid Zone
context.name: AM
context.definition: The AM zone is the central and semi-central zone immediately in front of the opponent's last line of defence. It is bordered to the back by the CM zone & to the front by the in-behind central zone. Horizontally, it is located inside the opponent's formation. Laterally, it borders the in-behind wide zones.
context.meaning: Central zone directly in front of the opponent's defensive line.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: In-Behind Wide Right Zone
context.name: IBWR
context.definition: The in-behind right zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the right side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide right zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: In-Behind Wide Left Zone
context.name: IBWL
context.definition: The in-behind left zone is behind or next to the opponent's defensive line. At least two opposing centre-backs can still defend the goal, at least one full-back has been bypassed. To the left side, the zone borders the AM zone and the in-behind central zone. From behind it borders the wide left zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a wide area with at least one full back bypassed.

parentKpi.label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
parentKpi.name: BALL_LOSS_REMOVED_TEAMMATES
parentKpi.definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by losing the ball, as none of them are between the ball and the goal.
parentKpi.meaning: Losing the ball in dangerous & vulnerable areas, turnovers in the build-up stage, enabling the opponent to counterattack.

context.label: In-Behind Central Zone
context.name: IB
context.definition: The in-behind central zone is the area behind the opponent's defence. An action here often represents a complete breakthrough on goal. There is a maximum of one opposing centre-back and the opposing goalkeeper between the ball and the goal. Vertically, you are within the boundaries of the opponent's formation. Laterally, the zone reaches the two furthest points of the defence (generally each full-back). To the rear, it borders the AM zone.
context.meaning: Tends to be a central space with at least one central defender bypassed.

context.label: Own Box
context.name: OWN_BOX
context.definition: The penalty box (16-metre zone) of the team currently in possession of the ball.
context.meaning: Own area

context.meaning: First third of the pitch without the box.

context.label: Right Wing

context.label: Right Half-Space
context.name: RIGHT_HALF_SPACE
context.definition: The half-space on the right from the point of view of the team currently in possession of the ball. This lane is bordered on the right by the edge of the box & to the left by the 6-yard-box.
context.meaning: Right half-space.

context.label: Centre
context.name: CENTER
context.definition: The centre of the pitch has the same width as the 6-yard-box.
context.meaning: Central lane of the pitch.
